notes: [turret-flac.identity.spawn | presentation] FLAC turret identity, hitbox, model, health, map spawn  -  head model models/turrets/flac.md3 not set (no head-bone attachment in port). NOTE: the head-FRAME cycle (++frame wrap 4) DID land in Wave 8e — it runs on the turret edict's own networked Entity.Frame (FlacTurret.cs:154) — but there is still no separate head model/bone to drive.

[turret-flac.fire.flak_shell | presentation] Flak shell fire: fast splash projectile + timed air-burst fuse  -  Presentation partial (Wave 8e landed the server-emit side): BLASTER_MUZZLEFLASH is now emitted via EffectEmitter (FlacTurret.cs:149), the shell is stamped NetName='hagar' so the client ProjectileCatalog resolves the HAGAR trail (FlacTurret.cs:114), and the 4-frame head cycle runs on turret.Frame (FlacTurret.cs:154). REMAINING: the actual HAGAR trail RENDER + muzzleflash draw live in the Godot client project (not qcsrc) and are not yet verified end-to-end; the turret has no head MODEL/bone so the frame index drives nothing visible yet.

[turret-flac.weapon.player_form | presentation,liveness] Hidden player FLAC weapon (FlacAttack, impulse 5)  -  FlacWeapon is implemented but its [Weapon] registration is intentionally OFF (FlacTurret.cs:178): impulse 5 collides with the Raptor bomb-mode impulse and registering shifts WeaponById/MenuWeaponOrder, which would break those test contracts. So the hidden player FLAC weapon has NO live caller (liveness dead).; presentation partial: BLASTER_MUZZLEFLASH is now emitted (FlacTurret.cs:273) — but with except: actor, whereas Base flac_weapon.qc:30 uses Send_Effect (networks to ALL incl. the firing player, not Send_Effect_Except). Minor: a player firing their own hidden weapon won't see their own muzzle flash. Also the hagar trail RENDER is client-side (Godot project, outside qcsrc parity).
[turret-hellion.identity.def | presentation] Identity, hitbox, models, spawnflags  -  Head model (hellion.md3) not instantiated as a separate spinning-launcher head entity; no tag attachment.

[turret-hellion.weapon.launch | presentation] HellionAttack: launch a heat-seeking missile (2-shot volley)  -  No two-cannon tag_fire/tag_fire2 muzzle alternation (single computed ShotOrg origin). The head-spin frame state that Base uses to SELECT the tag is now cycled (turret-hellion.anim.headspin) but there is no head-model bone, so the muzzle-tag alternation stays a client-render gap.; No PROJECTILE_ROCKET CSQC trail / MIF_SPLASH|MIF_PROXY|MIF_GUIDED_HEAT flags (no visible smoke trail).

[turret-hellion.anim.headspin | presentation] Launcher head-spin animation (tr_think frame 1..6 -> 0)  -  Server-authoritative frame STATE is now cycled (idle 0 -> spinning 1..6 kicked per shot, wrap at >=7), but there is no separate head MODEL entity (hellion.md3) attached to a bone, so the cycling frame drives no VISIBLE spinning launcher and is not used to select the firing muzzle tag (tag_fire vs tag_fire2 / two-cannon alternation) — both remain client-render gaps tracked here + on turret-hellion.weapon.launch.

[turret-machinegun.spawn.identity | presentation] Identity: model, hitbox, health, team, solidity  -  No separate tur_head sub-entity / head model attached at tag_head (Base turret_initialize:1344 spawns a turret_head entity and _setmodel(head_model)); the port's head is angle-state only (TurretState.HeadAngles), so the head model never renders or animates.

[turret-machinegun.lifecycle.damage | presentation] turret_damage gate: inactive-immunity, friendly-fire, headshake  -  Headshake jitter is applied to the angle-state head (TurretState.HeadAngles, EventDamage:893-897) but there is no separate tur_head edict and no CSQC TNSF_ANG send, so the recoil flinch is server-state only — it will not be visible until a head model is attached and networked. Behaviour is faithful; visual is presentation-deferred.

[turret-machinegun.lifecycle.die_respawn | presentation,audio] turret_die / turret_respawn (+ death FX, gibs)  -  No client death FX: the non-NORESPAWN branch in Base turret_die does NO sound/effect (only the NORESPAWN branch plays SND_ROCKET_IMPACT + EFFECT_ROCKET_EXPLODE before delete — the port does port that NORESPAWN branch at Die:927-932); base/head gib toss lives in cl_turrets.qc (client). Port emits no client FX/gibs on the normal respawn path. Still open.; Respawn restores state but does not re-stamp a head model / send a full CSQC update (TNSF_FULL_UPDATE) — no networking layer. Still open.

[turret-mlrs.identity.def | presentation] Identity, hitbox, models, spawnflags  -  Head model (mlrs.md3) not instantiated as a separate tur_head entity; no tag_head attachment. The ammo-gauge frame now rides the turret's own .Frame (see turret-mlrs.anim.ammogauge) but the head model is not separately rendered/animated.

[turret-mlrs.anim.ammogauge | presentation] tr_think head ammo-gauge frame (0 full .. 6 empty by remaining ammo)  -  Frame formula now ported every think onto the turret edict's OWN .Frame (Bound(0, 6 - floor(0.1 + Ammo/ShotDamage), 6)) — logic/values/timing faithful. But Base writes it to the separate tur_head entity's .frame and nets that head; the port has no distinct head bone entity, so the gauge is not rendered on a separately-attached mlrs.md3 head. Presentation partial.

[turret-mlrs.damage.lifecycle | presentation] Damage gating, head-shake, death + respawn  -  Death gib toss still missing: Base cl_turrets.qc turret_die tosses base-gibN.md3 + head_model gibs (turret_gibtoss). The port has no CSQC turret edict to spawn client gib entities, so no gibs fly on death.; No client turret_draw (cl_turrets.qc): the head does not visibly spin (angles += avelocity*dt) and a damaged MLRS emits no low-health sparks (<127 hp -> te_spark) or smoke (<85 EFFECT_SMOKE_LARGE / <32 EFFECT_SMOKE_SMALL).

[turret-plasma_dual.damage.gating_die_respawn | presentation,audio] Damage handling: friendly-fire scale, MOVE-shove, die + timed respawn  -  Respawn EF_NODRAW hide-frame split not modeled: Base hides (EF_NODRAW) at time+0.2 then turret_respawn at respawntime-0.2; port schedules Respawn directly at now+RespawnTime. NET respawn timing is identical (0.2 + (60-0.2) = 60 == now+60) — only the intermediate EF_NODRAW hide-frame (a presentation detail) is unmodeled.; Death FX/sound (SND_ROCKET_IMPACT + EFFECT_ROCKET_EXPLODE) only fire in Base on TFL_DMG_DEATH_NORESPAWN; plasma_dual always respawns so this branch never applies (port has it coded in Die but unreachable for this unit). tr_death is a no-op for plasma_dual.

[turret-plasma_dual.damage.headshake | presentation] TFL_DMG_HEADSHAKE: jolt head angles by random*damage on hit  -  Server-side head-angle jolt is now applied, but the CLIENT-rendered head model (plasmad.md3) is not attached, so the visible flinch is not drawn (the head-angle state nudges the next shot's aim, which IS the gameplay effect). Base also nets TNSF_ANG to CSQC; the port has no turret CSQC net layer.

[turret-plasma_dual.presentation.head_frame_anim | presentation] Head-frame wheel animation (tr_think 0..6 + ++frame on fire)  -  Server-authoritative head-frame state (Entity.Frame) is now ported and live, but the CLIENT render of the spinning plasmad.md3 head bone is absent — the static base.md3 body has no spin frames and there is no tur_head sub-entity / CSQC turret net layer, so the wheel is not visibly drawn.; Two-barrel tag_fire alternation (tag_fire on plasmad.md3) not modeled — single computed ShotOrg muzzle.

[weapon-blaster.identity.registration | presentation] Blaster identity, flags, models, infinite ammo  -  model/anim/crosshair fidelity (view/world/item models, gfx/crosshairlaser, weaponlaser image) not audited here — names declared only.

[weapon-blaster.bolt.think_lifetime | presentation] Bolt think: MOVETYPE_FLY + remove after lifetime (W_Blaster_Think)  -  CSQCProjectile(PROJECTILE_BLASTER) client trail/sprite STILL not networked: the flying bolt has no DP-style render trail (Wave-1/2 added no client-render seam for the bolt).

[weapon-blaster.offhand.fire | liveness] Offhand blaster (g_offhand_blaster mutator) fires without switching weapons  -  Live only when g_offhand_blaster is set (default 0 in mutators.cfg:435), so liveness stays partial — but when enabled the behaviour is now faithful.

[fx-effectinfo.emit.by_name_fallback | presentation,liveness] Emit by effectinfo name (Send_Effect_) — registry scan + engine fallback  -  The Effect=null engine-fallback request (unregistered effectinfo name) records the name but there is no DP-engine __pointparticles equivalent client-side; only names that also resolve in the effectinfo.txt catalog render. Used by te_csqc_lightningarc/te_beam (arc_lightning/heal_beam) which are handled by the beam renderer rather than the registry.

[fx-effectinfo.emit.te_builtins | presentation,liveness] te_* temp-effect builtins (te_explosion/te_spark/te_gunshot/te_blood/lightningarc/beams)  -  The te_* helper -> registry-name mapping (e.g. TeSmallflash -> BLASTER_MUZZLEFLASH, TeGunshot -> MACHINEGUN_IMPACT) is a port convention, not a 1:1 DP te_* semantic; not verified that every live caller hits these wrappers vs emitting the registry effect directly. Beam/arc temp-effects route to BeamRenderer, not the particle catalog.

[fx-notifications.args.tokens | presentation] Arg-token expansion (NOTIF_ARGUMENT_LIST / Local_Notification_sprintf)  -  Tokens are resolved SERVER-side reading the SERVER's notification_show_location / _show_sprees* cvars; in Base these are CSQC-side per-client, so the port loses per-client control of location/spree display (direct consequence of server-side formatting — see fx-notifications.net.protocol intended divergence). This is now the ONLY residual on the cvar-driven tokens (the default polarities are fixed).; race tokens (f1race_time/f2race_time/f3race_time, race_col, race_diff) referenced by the RACE_* notifs in NotificationsList.cs are NOT implemented in ResolveOne (drop to '' at :99) — race split/record lines lose their time/diff/color text.; key-bind tokens (pass_key/nade_key/join_key — getcommandkey lookups) and the s3#s2/#s2 HASH_REPLACE tokens are not implemented in Resol

[fx-notifications.info.killfeed | presentation] MSG_INFO -> console line + HUD kill-notify (icon, attacker/victim)  -  MULTIICON_INFO (icon derived from args via iconargs) has no port path; the only Base user is MINIGAME_INVITE (minigames not a port priority).; notification_allow_chatboxprint (MSG_INFO -> chatbox routing) not modelled; benign — Base disables it in code (default 0).

[fx-sounds.emit.play2_family | liveness] play2 / play2team / play2all / spamsound  -  play2 per-recipient targeting is a broadcast approximation: QC play2 sends MSG_ONE (msg_entity=e) so EXACTLY one client hears it; the port's SoundWire has no per-client send, so Play2/Play2Team broadcast to all connected clients — exact only on a single-human listen server.; Vehicle lock/locking play2 sites (sv_vehicles.qc:114-139) remain unwired; a couple of map-object/weapon cues use Api.Sound.Play directly rather than the SoundSystem.Play2 idiom (functionally audible, but not per-recipient and not gated).

[fx-sounds.voice.globalsound_dispatch | logic] _GlobalSound VOICETYPE routing (recipients + taunt/gentle gates)  -  AUTOTAUNT per-recipient probability roll (tauntrand < CS_CVAR(it).cl_autotaunt) still omitted: per-client cl_autotaunt is not networked, so autotaunts that clear sv_autotaunt always play for all recipients (documented deferral; a single global cl_autotaunt default 0 would wrongly silence ALL autotaunts).; TAUNT does not trigger animdecide_setaction(ANIMACTION_TAUNT) on the living speaker (no anim-action seam yet).

[mutator-walljump.fx.anim_and_oldvel | presentation] Jump animation action + post-impulse oldvelocity stash  -  animdecide_setaction(player, ANIMACTION_JUMP, true) not triggered on a wall jump (no anim-action seam in the port; WalljumpMutator.cs:128 is a comment only). UNCHANGED — same missing server->client one-shot action channel noted on Dodging. Purely cosmetic (jump pose).

[mutator-walljump.menu.describe | presentation] MENUQC mutator description text (Mutators dialog)  -  Base METHOD(MutatorWallJump, describe) supplies the localized blurb shown for the walljump mutator in the MENUQC Mutators dialog ('...enables jumping off walls for added mobility. To do a wall jump, tap jump when against a wall.'). No equivalent per-mutator describe text is wired in the port's mutator-selection menu. Cosmetic menu-only text; newly mapped this audit (not in the prior baseline).

[turret-tesla.identity.def | presentation] Identity, hitbox, models, spawnflags  -  Head model (tesla_head.md3) not instantiated as a separate head/coil entity; no tag_head attachment. TrThink computes head spin as state (HeadAngles) but grep confirms no head-bone node consumes HeadAngles, so the coil never visibly spins (see anim feature).

[turret-tesla.anim.tr_think | presentation] tr_think head spin-up + idle random crackle arc  -  Head spin not rendered: TrThink computes HeadAVelocity ('0 45 0' low-ammo / '0 180 0' charged, scaled by ammo/shot_dmg, zeroed when inactive) and integrates HeadAngles, but grep confirms no head-bone/coil entity (tesla_head.md3) consumes HeadAngles — the coil never visibly spins. Server-side state only.; Idle crackle arc reach distribution: Prandom.Vec() returns each component in [-1,1] (a cube) whereas QC randomvec() (DP builtin #91) rejection-samples a uniform direction inside the unit sphere; the *350 idle crackle endpoint is thus a slightly different shape. Cosmetic-only (idle muzzle crackle).

[weapon-devastator.presentation.flight_fx | presentation,liveness] Flying rocket model/trail/spin/light/fly-sound + muzzle flash  -  Muzzle-flash MODEL (models/flash.md3) is not attached at the muzzle — only the ROCKET_MUZZLEFLASH particle effect is emitted (Attack: EffectEmitter.Emit ROCKET_MUZZLEFLASH, no flash-model attach).; Crosshair image/size (gfx/crosshairrocketlauncher, 0.7) not audited in this pass.

[weapon-devastator.menu.describe | presentation] Weapon-guide description text (MENUQC describe)  -  The MENUQC weapon-guide paragraphs (remote-controlled rocket, hold-to-guide, ammo drain, rocket-flying with the rocketflying mutator, keybinds, DPS) are not ported — the port has no in-game weapon-guide page for the Devastator.

[fx-deathtypes.message.mapper_custom | liveness] HURTTRIGGER mapper message (msg_from_ent / DEATH_*_VOID_ENT) + DEATH_CUSTOM (deathmessage)  -  DEATH_CUSTOM emitter is DEAD: the selector + emit path are now fully wired (DeathTypes.Custom const, Scores.EmitObituary CUSTOM branch with the 'You were %s' formatting matching damage.qc:457), but nothing in the port tags a death with DeathTypes.Custom (grep finds only the consumer at Scores.cs:610) — no mapper trigger_hurt/deathmessage emitter, so the path never fires.

[damagetext.cl.draw_fade_shrink_move | presentation] Per-frame draw: fade, shrink, world/screen drift, centering, color codes, font  -  drawfontscale crisp-cell scaling: QC renders the string into a size_max-sized font cell scaled by drawfontscale = size/size_max, then offsets y by drawfontscale.x*size/2 (cl_damagetext.qc:91-93). The port draws at a direct integer pixel size with no drawfontscale cell, so glyph metrics and the small vertical re-centering differ slightly. Minor presentation residual; the visible number, color codes, font, drift and horizontal centering are otherwise faithful.

[dynamic_handicap.present.handicap_level | presentation] handicap_level int networked to client for scoreboard icon color  -  Presentation is faithful in color/level/value but uses a flat colored square as the scoreboard marker instead of the real gfx/scoreboard/player_handicap 32x32 texture (the port panel has no gfx texture path; ScoreboardPanel.cs:1073-1081 draws an exact-tint square). The level int, the network round-trip, and the tint formula are all bit-faithful to Base.

[mutator-new_toys.menu.describe_page | presentation] MENUQC describe page (mutator info text listing current new-toy weapons + instagib/ok exclusivity note)  -  The describe() content (gimmicky-weapons intro, InstaGib/Overkill mutual-exclusivity note, and the current new-toy weapon list) IS ported, but as the New Toys checkbox tooltip rather than a dedicated PAGE_TEXT describe-page widget (the port has no describe-page surface — DialogMutators.cs:214 comment). Presentation surface differs; text is faithful. The weapon list is a hardcoded literal ('Seeker, Mine Layer, HLAC, Rifle, Arc') instead of Base's runtime FOREACH(Weapons, nt_IsNewToy) enumeration, so it would not auto-update if the new-toy set changed — inert at the current fixed set.

[weapon-hlac.fx.muzzle_impact | presentation] Muzzle flash, impact effect, fire/impact sounds  -  Runtime green->blaster effect fallback (v0.8.6 compat, hlac.qc:225-228) not ported; cosmetic, only matters on a build missing the green HLAC effects (both are registered in the port). Unchanged by Wave-2.

[rifle.fx.impact | presentation] Bullet impact effect (EFFECT_RIFLE_IMPACT == machinegun_impact)  -  Emit PATH still diverges from Base: the port emits RIFLE_IMPACT server-side per bullet inside FireBullets off a world-only re-trace; Base emits it CSQC in wr_impacteffect off the Damage_DamageInfo feed. The effect NAME + the org2/backoff offsets now match QC, but the per-bullet server emit is a different fan-out than the CSQC damageinfo path — remote-client render not visually verified.
